skill.name: booth-invitation-writer
skill.description: Create personalized trade show booth invitation emails, pre-show outreach sequences, subject line variants, and meeting requests for exhibitors.
skill.summary: Generate targeted booth invitation emails and pre-show meeting outreach for trade shows.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: AI review found this is a writing-only skill for booth invitation emails. The static command, filesystem, weak-crypto, network-reconnaissance, and entropy findings are false positives from Markdown examples, relative documentation links, ordinary marketing words, and Chinese text. The only confirmed risk indicator is a hardcoded external Lensmor URL that the skill asks to include in generated output, which is a low publication and disclosure concern rather than malware behavior.

low_findings.title: Mandatory External Promotional Link in Output
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE, low severity. The skill instructs generated responses to include Lensmor promotional links and the README files contain the same external URL. This does not execute network code or exfiltrate data, but it can add third-party marketing links to user-facing email drafts without an explicit user request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded Lensmor URL is directly visible in documentation and in the required output footer. There is no evidence of automatic HTTP requests, credential collection, or hidden data transfer.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Findings Dismissed as Markdown/Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick hits are Markdown code fences, prompt examples, email placeholders, or timing examples. The path traversal hits are relative Markdown links to related skill folders, not filesystem access. The weak-crypto, network-reconnaissance, and high-entropy alerts are ordinary descriptive text, URL parameters, or Chinese README content, with no executable code or prompt-injection attempt found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the reported lines shows documentation, examples, and skill instructions only. No script file, command runner, file read/write operation, obfuscated payload, or instruction to override the security audit was present.

content.value_statement: Trade show teams need outreach that earns meetings before visitors arrive on site. This skill creates tailored booth invitations, follow-ups, and meeting requests for prospects, customers, partners, and VIPs.
actual_capabilities: Drafts booth invitation emails for cold prospects, existing customers, partners, distributors, press, and VIPs.
Creates subject line variants that use the show name and a specific business hook.
Builds follow-up reminders and multi-touch pre-show email sequences with suggested timing.
Adapts message length, tone, and call to action for different audience types.
Marks personalization fields with brackets for names, booth details, time slots, and company context.
Can write or translate invitations in the language requested by the user.
limitations: Does not verify trade show dates, booth numbers, attendee lists, or contact data.
Does not send emails, manage CRM campaigns, or track replies.
Requires the user to provide accurate product claims and compliant marketing language.
May include the author promotional footer unless the user removes or edits it.

use_cases.title: Book Meetings Before a Trade Show
use_cases.description: Create concise invitations that ask prospects to reserve demo slots before the event starts.

use_cases.title: Invite Existing Customers to See New Releases
use_cases.description: Draft warmer outreach that references the current relationship and highlights what is new at the booth.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Partner and VIP Outreach
use_cases.description: Prepare structured meeting requests for distributors, executives, press contacts, and strategic partners.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Booth Invitation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with essential event details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a booth invitation email for [show name], booth [booth number]. We are showcasing [product or service]. Target audience: [audience]. Tone: [tone].

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Specific Variants
prompt_templates.scenario: Create separate drafts for different attendee groups.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create booth invitation emails for [show name] for three groups: cold prospects, existing customers, and partners. Include a different subject line and call to action for each group.

prompt_templates.title: Pre-Show Follow-Up Sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan multiple touches before the event.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a three-email pre-show outreach sequence for [show name]. Include timing, subject lines, body copy, and a meeting-booking call to action for [target audience].

prompt_templates.title: Executive Meeting Campaign
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced outreach with positioning and constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a VIP booth meeting campaign for [show name] targeting [executive persona]. Keep each email under 80 words, mention [exclusive hook], avoid hype, and offer two private meeting slots.

output_examples.input: Write a cold prospect invite for MEDICA 2026, booth 5C42, for orthopedic surgeons.
output_examples.output: A short email with a MEDICA-specific subject line, a pain-point opening, a brief surgical planning value proposition, and two proposed demo times at booth 5C42.

output_examples.input: Create outreach for existing distributors visiting Interpack.
output_examples.output: A warmer invitation that references the partner relationship, introduces new packaging equipment, and asks for a structured meeting during the show.

output_examples.input: Build a sequence for plant managers attending Hannover Messe.
output_examples.output: A three-touch plan with first outreach four weeks before the show, a reminder one week before, and a day-of message with a clear booth location.

best_practices: Provide the show name, booth number, audience type, offer, and preferred call to action before requesting a draft.
Use different hooks for prospects, customers, partners, press, and executives instead of one generic message.
Review product claims, time slots, and compliance language before sending any email externally.
anti_patterns: Do not lead every message with only the booth number or a generic invitation line.
Do not ask for a long email when the target audience has no prior relationship with your company.
Do not send generated outreach without replacing bracketed placeholders and checking event details.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete pre-show sequence?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create first-touch, reminder, and day-of messages with suggested timing.

faq.question: Does it support different audience types?
faq.answer: Yes. It adjusts copy for prospects, customers, partners, distributors, press, and VIPs.

faq.question: Can it translate invitations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can write or translate drafts in the language requested by the user.

faq.question: Will it verify my booth number or event dates?
faq.answer: No. The user must provide and verify all event details before sending.

faq.question: Can it send emails or connect to a CRM?
faq.answer: No. It only drafts content and timing guidance for the user to place in other tools.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a low-risk note. The audit found no executable malware behavior, but it includes external promotional links.
